title: bupleurum jumceum subsp. gerardii
intro: Bupleurum jumceum subsp. gerardii, also known as Bupleurum rotundifolium or small-leaved hare's ear, is a plant species within the genus Bupleurum, belonging to the family Apiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various forms of medicine, particularly in Chinese herbalism, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and immunomodulatory effects. The health benefits of Bupleurum jumceum subsp. gerardii include its potential to aid in the treatment of fever, headache, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the liver and immune system. In herbal preparations, Bupleurum jumceum subsp. gerardii is often combined with other herbs, such as glycyrrhiza and licorice root, to create teas, capsules, and tinctures that promote overall well-being and alleviate various health conditions.
